name: Universal Declaration of Human Rights (UDHR 1948)
summary: The Universal Declaration of Human Rights (UDHR) is a global supranational meta-framework foundational human-rights soft-law instrument adopted by the UN General Assembly — structurally distinctive globally as the foundational modern international human-rights instrument framework underlying all subsequent modern international + regional human-rights frameworks (UDHR adopted 10 December 1948 by UN General Assembly Resolution 217 A (III) in Palais de Chaillot, Paris — formal foundational human-rights soft-law instrument framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-human-rights frameworks which operate as binding-treaty frameworks subsequent to + influenced by UDHR framework, structurally distinctive globally as the foundational modern international human-rights instrument framework underlying all subsequent modern international + regional human-rights binding-treaty frameworks), as the most-translated document in human history (UDHR has been translated into 530+ languages as of 2026 — formal Guinness World Record as most-translated document in human history, structurally distinctive globally as the most-translated document in human history), as the central jurisdiction of the formal Article 16 family-rights framework (UDHR Article 16 framework on rights to marry and found a family + family-rights framework — formal foundational family-rights framework underlying all subsequent modern international + regional family-rights frameworks, structurally distinctive globally as the most-fundamental modern family-rights framework provision), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal post-WWII Eleanor Roosevelt-chaired UN Commission on Human Rights elaboration framework (UDHR drafted 1946-1948 by UN Commission on Human Rights chaired by Eleanor Roosevelt — formal UN drafting process framework, structurally distinctive globally as the formal foundational human-rights drafting process framework underlying all subsequent modern international human-rights frameworks). The UDHR is binding upon all UN member states as customary international law framework, with substantial debate on extent of binding-customary-international-law framework. The Declaration is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal Article 16 family-rights framework operates substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework. The UDHR has no formal monitoring body framework — implementation framework operates via subsequent UN treaty bodies + Special Procedures + International Court of Justice frameworks. The UDHR is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.title: Universal Declaration of Human Rights
statutory_framework.relevance: Universal Declaration of Human Rights adopted 10 December 1948 by UN General Assembly Resolution 217 A (III) in Palais de Chaillot, Paris — formal foundational human-rights soft-law instrument framework.

statutory_framework.citation: UDHR Article 16 (Rights to Marry and Found a Family)
statutory_framework.title: UDHR Article 16
statutory_framework.relevance: UDHR Article 16 framework on rights to marry and found a family + family-rights framework — formal foundational family-rights framework underlying all subsequent modern international + regional family-rights frameworks.

statutory_framework.title: UDHR Article 25(2)
statutory_framework.relevance: UDHR Article 25(2) framework providing 'Motherhood and childhood are entitled to special care and assistance. All children, whether born in or out of wedlock, shall enjoy the same social protection' — formal foundational children's-rights framework.

statutory_framework.citation: International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights ICCPR 1966 + International Covenant on Economic, Social and Cultural Rights ICESCR 1966
statutory_framework.title: ICCPR + ICESCR
statutory_framework.relevance: International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights ICCPR + International Covenant on Economic, Social and Cultural Rights ICESCR adopted 16 December 1966 + effective 23 March 1976 + 3 January 1976 — formal binding-treaty translation of UDHR framework, formally constituting International Bill of Human Rights framework alongside UDHR.

apex_courts.name: International Court of Justice (ICJ)
apex_courts.role: International Court of Justice — formal global judicial body, including formal jurisdiction over UDHR framework as customary international law framework.

apex_courts.name: UN Treaty Bodies (UNCRC + CEDAW + CRPD + CCPR + CESCR + others)
apex_courts.role: UN Treaty Bodies — formal monitoring framework bodies for subsequent UN binding-treaty frameworks based on UDHR framework.

anonymisation_convention: UDHR-related individual communications operate via subsequent UN treaty bodies + Special Procedures frameworks using initials.

key_developments.title: UN founded + UN Charter adopted
key_developments.description: United Nations founded 24 October 1945 + UN Charter adopted 26 June 1945 — beginning of formal UN regional supranational framework.

key_developments.title: UN Commission on Human Rights established
key_developments.description: UN Commission on Human Rights established February 1946 — formal UN human-rights drafting framework + UDHR drafting process framework.

key_developments.title: UDHR adopted
key_developments.description: Universal Declaration of Human Rights adopted 10 December 1948 by UN General Assembly Resolution 217 A (III) in Palais de Chaillot, Paris — formal foundational human-rights soft-law instrument framework.

key_developments.title: ICCPR + ICESCR adopted
key_developments.description: International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights ICCPR + International Covenant on Economic, Social and Cultural Rights ICESCR adopted 16 December 1966 — formal binding-treaty translation of UDHR framework.

key_developments.title: ICCPR + ICESCR enter force
key_developments.description: ICCPR entered force 23 March 1976 + ICESCR entered force 3 January 1976 — formal International Bill of Human Rights framework operational.

key_developments.title: UDHR Guinness World Record
key_developments.description: UDHR achieved Guinness World Record as most-translated document in human history 2009 — formal global recognition of UDHR translation framework.

structural_findings: UDHR operates a supranational global foundational human-rights soft-law framework — places UDHR in the global supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Foundational modern international human-rights instrument framework underlying all subsequent modern international + regional human-rights frameworks is structurally distinctive globally — formal foundational human-rights soft-law instrument framework.
Most-translated document in human history is structurally distinctive globally — Guinness World Record + 530+ languages as of 2026.

Central jurisdiction of formal Article 16 family-rights framework is structurally distinctive globally — formal foundational family-rights framework underlying all subsequent modern international + regional family-rights frameworks.

Central jurisdiction of formal post-WWII Eleanor Roosevelt-chaired UN Commission on Human Rights elaboration framework is structurally distinctive globally — formal foundational human-rights drafting process framework underlying all subsequent modern international human-rights frameworks.

Customary international law framework status is structurally distinctive globally — soft-law framework operating as customary international law framework with substantial debate on binding-extent framework.
Article 16 + Article 25(2) formal family-rights + children's-rights dual framework is structurally distinctive globally.
1948 UDHR + 1966 ICCPR + ICESCR + 1976 entry into force International-Bill-of-Human-Rights-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.
editorial_notes: UDHR jurisdiction sidecar — supranational global foundational human-rights soft-law meta-framework (UDHR 1948 + Article 16 Family Rights + Article 25(2) Children's Special Care + UN Charter 1945 + UN Commission on Human Rights 1946 + ICCPR 1966/1976 + ICESCR 1966/1976 + International Bill of Human Rights). Foundational modern international human-rights instrument framework underlying all subsequent modern international + regional human-rights frameworks globally + most-translated document in human history + central jurisdiction of formal Article 16 family-rights framework + central jurisdiction of formal post-WWII Eleanor Roosevelt-chaired UN Commission on Human Rights elaboration framework + Customary international law framework status + Article 16 + Article 25(2) formal family-rights + children's-rights dual framework + 1948 UDHR + 1966 ICCPR + ICESCR + 1976 entry into force International-Bill-of-Human-Rights-framework-development sequence.
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.

Joins global + supranational + foundational-human-rights-soft-law cluster + UDHR-foundational-modern-international-human-rights-instrument-globally-distinctive + most-translated-document-in-human-history + Article-16-Family-Rights-foundational + Article-25-2-Children-Special-Care-foundational + Eleanor-Roosevelt-UN-Commission-on-Human-Rights-drafting-framework + customary-international-law-framework-status + International-Bill-of-Human-Rights-UDHR-ICCPR-ICESCR-1948-1966-1976 clusters within the corpus.
